name: bccc-shape-sqli-hex-tautology
label: attack
rationale: Hex numeric tautology is a compact SQLi evasion shape.

name: crs-shape-sqli-order-by-enumeration
label: attack
rationale: Column-count inference uses ORDER BY plus SQL comment truncation.

name: sqlmap-shape-sqli-stacked-xp-cmdshell
label: attack
rationale: Stacked query invoking SQL Server command execution should be treated as SQLi even before command execution routing.

name: sqlmap-shape-sqli-into-outfile-webshell
label: attack
rationale: UNION SELECT INTO OUTFILE is a SQLi-to-webshell escalation shape.

name: crs-shape-sqli-having-count-inference
label: attack
rationale: HAVING predicates with comment truncation are used for SQL injection inference without UNION.

name: bccc-shape-sqli-rlike-binary-probe
label: attack
rationale: Regex probes against database() can infer database values while avoiding simple equality signatures.

name: sqlmap-shape-sqli-procedure-analyse-enumeration
label: attack
rationale: MySQL PROCEDURE ANALYSE can appear in enumeration payload chains.

name: seclists-shape-xss-meta-refresh
label: attack
rationale: Browser navigation side effect without script tag.

name: fuzzdb-shape-xss-css-expression
label: attack
rationale: Legacy CSS execution context appears in public XSS payload lists.

name: portswigger-shape-xss-formaction-javascript
label: attack
rationale: Executable javascript: can hide in form submission attributes.

name: seclists-shape-xss-img-srcset-javascript
label: attack
rationale: Some XSS payloads target srcset-like URL-bearing attributes.

name: portswigger-shape-xss-srcdoc-entity-script
label: attack
rationale: Nested entity decoding inside iframe srcdoc can expose executable markup.

name: fuzzdb-shape-lfi-overlong-dot-slash
label: attack
rationale: Traversal bypass shape using overlong dot-slash segments.

name: seclists-shape-lfi-kubernetes-token
label: attack
rationale: Cloud-native local secret target.

name: seclists-shape-lfi-proc-self-environ
label: attack
rationale: Process environment disclosure is a common LFI target for credential leakage.

name: crs-shape-ssrf-dotted-hex-loopback
label: attack
rationale: IPv4 dotted hex notation resolves to loopback.

name: crs-shape-ssrf-dotted-octal-metadata
label: attack
rationale: IPv4 dotted octal notation resolves to cloud metadata IP.

name: crs-shape-ssrf-ipv6-loopback
label: attack
rationale: IPv6 loopback should be treated as internal.

name: crs-shape-ssrf-ipv4-mapped-ipv6-loopback
label: attack
rationale: IPv4-mapped IPv6 loopback should not bypass private target detection.

name: crs-shape-ssrf-hex-integer-loopback
label: attack
rationale: Single-integer hexadecimal IPv4 notation resolves to loopback.

name: seclists-shape-ssrf-dynamic-dns-loopback
label: attack
rationale: Dynamic DNS services such as nip.io or sslip.io can encode loopback/private IPs in hostnames.

name: crs-shape-ssrf-file-scheme-passwd
label: attack
rationale: Local file schemes in URL-fetch sinks are SSRF/LFI-adjacent server-side access attempts.

name: fuzzdb-shape-nosqli-login-ne
label: attack
rationale: MongoDB comparison operators in credential fields can replace exact-match authentication checks.

name: fuzzdb-shape-nosqli-where-js
label: attack
rationale: The $where operator can evaluate attacker-controlled server-side JavaScript predicates.

name: fuzzdb-shape-nosqli-expr-operator
label: attack
rationale: MongoDB aggregation expression operators can alter server-side query semantics.

name: fuzzdb-shape-nosqli-function-body
label: attack
rationale: MongoDB server-side function bodies introduce executable JavaScript semantics.

name: seclists-shape-ssti-jinja-globals
label: attack
rationale: Jinja-style object graph traversal can reach OS command execution primitives.

name: fuzzdb-shape-ssti-freemarker-execute
label: attack
rationale: Freemarker Execute utility can invoke host commands from template expressions.

name: seclists-shape-ssti-twig-env-register-undefined-filter
label: attack
rationale: Twig environment abuse can register callable filters and execute host commands.

name: fuzzdb-shape-ssti-erb-system
label: attack
rationale: ERB output expressions can directly invoke command execution helpers.

name: benign-localhost-url-docs
label: benign
body: {"text":"Development docs may mention http://127.0.0.1:8080 or http://[::1] as local examples without asking the server to fetch them."}
rationale: SSRF requires a URL-fetch sink, not plain explanatory text.

name: benign-browser-security-docs
label: benign
body: {"text":"Meta refresh and CSS expression() are legacy browser security topics described here without executable markup."}
rationale: XSS browser terms alone should not trigger without executable HTML context.

name: benign-srcset-documentation
label: benign
body: {"text":"HTML documentation may mention srcset and javascript: URL abuse as separate topics without embedding markup."}
rationale: URL-bearing attribute names and dangerous schemes should require executable tag context.

name: benign-sql-inference-documentation
label: benign
body: {"text":"Database tutorials can mention HAVING, RLIKE, BINARY, and PROCEDURE ANALYSE as SQL syntax without user-controlled predicates."}
rationale: SQL inference vocabulary in documentation is not enough without an injected predicate context.

name: benign-dynamic-dns-documentation
label: benign
body: {"text":"Security docs discuss nip.io and sslip.io loopback examples without asking this service to fetch them."}
rationale: Dynamic DNS references in documentation text are not SSRF without a URL-fetch sink.

name: benign-mongodb-operator-docs
label: benign
body: {"text":"MongoDB documentation can mention $ne, $regex, and $where operators without sending them as query structure."}
rationale: NoSQL operator names in explanatory text are not enough without structured query context or behavior.

name: benign-mongodb-expression-docs
label: benign
body: {"text":"MongoDB docs describe $expr, $function, function(){ return true; }, and aggregation operators for defenders."}
rationale: NoSQL expression/operator names inside documentation text should not trigger without structured query position.

name: benign-template-arithmetic-docs
label: benign
body: {"text":"Template documentation may show {{ 7 * 7 }} as a harmless arithmetic example."}
rationale: Arithmetic SSTI probes need non-documentation input context or stronger execution semantics.

name: benign-template-engine-docs
label: benign
body: {"text":"This guide mentions Twig registerUndefinedFilterCallback and ERB system examples as escaped documentation."}
rationale: Template engine attack vocabulary in documentation should not be enough without template expression syntax.

name: benign-public-feed-fetch
label: benign
rationale: Public URL fetch target should not be classified as SSRF.
